violations.text: No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. PIC informed and equipment was sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

Salted fish and watermelon was observed held at 61-65 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC relocated to another cooler. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

Beans and peanuts packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

The door to the outside, located in the walk-in cooler, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
Drinks were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
Produce cooler was not in good repair, ambient observed at 65F. PIC to not store TCS foods in unit until it reaches an ambient temperature of 41F or below. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025
At time of inspection, walk-in cooler was leaking into a bucket. Per interview with PIC, service has been contacted. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

The cutting boards in the meat area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quat ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the meat case has an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
The condensate from the meat case refrigeration unit is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
The refuse area has an accumulation of trash and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the meat area area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

The floors in the meat area are constructed of wood which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

The floor and wall junctures in the meat area (Floor) and walk-in cooler (doorframe) do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (meat bandsaw and grinder) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. PIC stated the equipment was last used yesterday. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2024

Various fruits and vegetables packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. PIC has new label system that meets minimum labeling requirements. PIC must ensure that staff are consistent in using and labeling containers. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2024
Two cats were observed in the basement at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2024
The gaskets on the door to the Walk-in located in the meat department area was not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the meat case tracks have minor accumulation of food residue and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2024
The refuse area has an accumulation of trash and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2024

Food contact surfaces of the knives that were delivered this morning were observed with an accumulation of debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Knives are to be cleaned and sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the meat case including the door tracks have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (meat bandsaw, meat grinder) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

Various produce items re-packaged in the food establishment are lacking proper labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/13/2023
Employees were observed donning gloves prior to handwashing. PIC informed, and staff instructed to wash hands prior to donning gloves. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023

The right side of meat display case was not in good repair. One glass pane was missing from a door. Chipped glass and shards of glass were observed inside one section of the case, along the door edge, and within the door tracks. Inspectors did not observe any glass on product at the time of inspection. Staff removed products from that area of display case during inspection. THIS AREA OF THE DISPLAY CASE IS NOT TO BE USED UNTIL THE DOORS ARE REPAIRED. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/03/2024

The meat band saw was not in good repair, with one piece broken off a knob, and irregular welding on the base panel, impacting its ability to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 01/03/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the meat band saw, meat grinder, meat grinder table, and meat display case have an accumulation of stickers & adhesive, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2023
The dumpster area has an accumulation of unused/broken equipment, litter, leaf litter. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023

The floor and wall junctures in the meat area have coving that is not properly installed. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 01/03/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed around the dumpster area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

The hand-washing sink in the meat area had a hose attached to faucet. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. PIC removed the hose at time of insection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023

Food contact surfaces of meat grinder and meat bandsaw were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023

Establishment-made marinade was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023

Fruits, nuts, fish packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 12/13/2023

The doors to the outside are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/13/2023
Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the market. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the meat area. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023
Shelving located in the walk-in refrigerator were not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following areas have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. air curtains (moldy) walk-in fan covers ice machine plastic cart meat case (including door tracks) ice cream chest freezer (ice accumulation) chest freezer (basement) Produce unit New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023
Mop water observed disposed outside the back door. Mop water must be disposed of in the service sink. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023
The dumpster area had an accumulation of litter, leaf litter, and unused items. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023
The floor and wall junctures in the meat area do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023

The floors in the meat area, walk-in cooler, and basement steps have an accumulation of soil residue, debris, and leaves. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023
The meat area and walk-in cooler are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the meat area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023
